item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All that really distinguishes this from a generic hivemind that much is the mention of expanding rooms in the record, though this is quickly thrown out of the window without development in favour of a run-of-the-mill aggressive hivemind in the incident log. This, when combined with the seemingly random other events, leads to something which doesn't come across as a coherent and interesting whole.

item.title: GLORY TO THIS MAN
item.content:encoded: MAY HE BE A GREAT LEADER OF OUR COUNTRY

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want to throw a shout out to Mortos and InsanitysBane for their input. I referenced both of their posts multiple times during the rewrite and sought to address as much of their concerns as I could while expanding what had been there before.

It's no longer just four short audio logs. I wrote an entire Experiment Log that basically tells a short story about what happened in Washington at Temporary Site 34-Gamma.

I welcome further input on the new addendum especially. It's probably really rough: I basically just wrote it and posted. I didn't know how to go about getting someone to read a rewrite. I think it still turned out all right though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The article doesn't state anywhere what SCP-3129's area of effect actually is. Is it just the continental United States? Has it appeared anywhere else outside the United States? Does SCP-3129 appear more often in places where the ideas of community and togetherness are "on the decline"? Or does it prefer places where those ideas are already strongly valued? How frequent are SCP-3129 infected broadcasts?

Good questions, thanks! I'll keep this in mind for the pending rewrite.

The description lead me to believe that SCP-3129-1 has specific ideas that people who already agree with make them more susceptible to infection, but the example log at the bottom seems to cite 3129-1's main ideas as security, togetherness, and community which are pretty board and popular ideas among any stock politician regardless of party, and ideas which include a pretty broad amount of potentially vulnerable infectees. Wouldn't that pool of possible cognitohazard-vectors as well as the incident report (which might suggest possible reality reconstruction or at least a perception-based effect) be a point of concern for the Foundation to at least consider a pending Keter classification?

It doesn't actually. The commercials are vague on purpose: "Mr November" doesn't actually understand politics. Its use of local politics is purely opportunistic. It saw a quick and dirty way to get what it needed (attention) and decided to emulate it. Badly. This thing's poor reproduction of actual campaign ads is entirely intentional. It doesn't even need to be actually convincing because the hazardous aspect of the transmissions is what gets it what it needs. The fact that it works on people of particular alignment at any given time is coincidental.

It doesn't really have ideas of its own as far as an actual "campaign" is concerned. Different commercials say different things. The entity just throws out what it thinks people want to hear and tries its best to carry the facade of an actual person.

As far as its classification is concerned, maybe a pending Keter designation is appropriate. This is just an unfortunate result of the end of the first addendum not aligning with the rest of the article, as pointed out by Mortos. That's the main goal of the rewrite. Whitch doesn't have a timeline right now because I have no idea what to write for it. It's been a long, difficult work week and I feel burnt out.

I won't let it sit as is for too much longer. If I can't get a rewrite done within a few days I'm gonna self-delete so I can work on it without worrying about the score of this one.

I don't wanna downvote, but I just feel like the ideas as they are presented here aren't fully developed and realized. If can address a few of these questions in some edits, I think I could confidently upvote this. Otherwise, this feels like it's almost something good but just not quite there yet.

Agreed. I have to rewrite the middle of this article. Thanks for your input.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The article doesn't state anywhere what SCP-3129's area of effect actually is. Is it just the continental United States? Has it appeared anywhere else outside the United States? Does SCP-3129 appear more often in places where the ideas of community and togetherness are "on the decline"? Or does it prefer places where those ideas are already strongly valued? How frequent are SCP-3129 infected broadcasts?

The description lead me to believe that SCP-3129-1 has specific ideas that people who already agree with make them more susceptible to infection, but the example log at the bottom seems to cite 3129-1's main ideas as security, togetherness, and community which are pretty board and popular ideas among any stock politician regardless of party, and ideas which include a pretty broad amount of potentially vulnerable infectees. Wouldn't that pool of possible cognitohazard-vectors as well as the incident report (which might suggest possible reality reconstruction or at least a perception-based effect) be a point of concern for the Foundation to at least consider a pending Keter classification?

I don't wanna downvote, but I just feel like the ideas as they are presented here aren't fully developed and realized. If can address a few of these questions in some edits, I think I could confidently upvote this. Otherwise, this feels like it's almost something good but just not quite there yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for your input. I was looking for places to improve and, after rereading the article a few dozen more times and then going back over the comments, you make a good point here. So I feel I need to:

  • Expand on what was gleaned from the Washington experiment. Might as well unredact the name of the town too (Bremerton). No reason for that.
  • Expand a little on the extent of the entity's ability to affect reality. It's not entirely clear what it can do or how. The closing statements of the debrief were never brought in line with what was written later.
  • Gonna delete a line or two based off of other input. I think I can get rid of the Foundation insignia line. I think it's better off implied.

Thanks for leaving a comment with your downvote. It helped.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is creepy, and distinct enough from 2078 that I didn't think it detracts from the anomalousity. It just needs attention - lots of attention from many people, all the time.

There are non-anomalous human beings like that, which adds to the impact. Some of them run for public office.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like how this has just enough to weird me out, and not too much to leave me guessing.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thoroughly enjoyed this!

I think there’s a small typo though (in the second paragraph of the description):

SCP-3129 phenomena invariably focus upon a human male, aged somewhere between 40 and 50, hereafter designated SCP-3129.

It sounds like this is supposed to be the 3129-1 designation, but you only put in 3129.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted, but however there is some things that I think should be expanded on. This is the beginning to a really good tale. You should see if you could add to the story. Also there is a site located in Indiana maybe you should switch 34 with 81 but thats just personal bias.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My problem with this is none of the stuff set up in the middle actually seems to go anywhere. The implication that buildings are building altered into convention halls or the like is never touched again, and the whole notion that they were going to perform a detailed inspection of Stage 3 infectees never goes anywhere besides a few short logs of people saying the same things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is also an issue for me. I quite like this article in isolation - it's well put together, straightforward while still having some mystery, and subtly creepy. Unfortunately, I didn't feel it quite distinguished itself from 2078, and I found 2078 to edge this out in the creepy department, with the final speech, so I novoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hadn't seen 2078. That's a big issue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very weird. The fact that he wants to be governor of Indiana despite affecting Washington was very unsettling. If it's a typo, don't change it.

I got a good Whovian vibe from this, but it's weirdly similar yet distinct to SCP-2078 that my upvote is not fully confident, but my mind also jumps to a possible tale connection.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Couple lingering XXXX's. Thought I saw a spacing error, somewhere in there.

Otherwise, pretty unsettling. The imagery of a couple guys wearing Foundation stars is pretty hamfisted - thought the point of black cloth was to prevent identification - but otherwise, it strung me along pretty well.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
My first SCP posted on the mainlist! Let's GO!

+ Thanks to
- Close

jabyrwock for their revisions!
DrChandra for their review!
Modern_Erasmus for their input on the initial concept!
The Great Hippo for their essay on LAUGH IS FUN and how to write a truly unsettling SCP.
hydrozen for their input!

+ Where did this come from?
- Close

My inspiration is 80% music. Music is my life. This article came from listening to "Mr. November" by The National. I fixated on the name of the song and thought it would make a good name for a skip.

The meat of the article was heavily inspired by The Great Hippo's essay as well as my tremendous fandom of Doctor Who. I tried for an overall "Whovian" tone of creepy curiosity and an entity that isn't particularly evil or whatever. It just needs attention.

The image is CC compliant and was found through Wikimedia Commons. One problem though: it's John Marchione, the mayor of Redmond Washington. That might be an issue I don't know.
